title: RNA Viruses and Hepatitis C — Self-Study

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Self-study page on RNA viruses with emphasis on hepatitis C virus (HCV) biology.

items.stem: Which type of RNA virus genome can be directly translated by host ribosomes without a transcription step?
Why is microRNA-122 essential for efficient hepatitis C virus replication in hepatocytes?

options.A: It stabilizes the viral 5' UTR and promotes RNA abundance
options.D: It integrates the viral genome into chromosome 22

items.stem: Which HCV protein is primarily responsible for RNA-dependent RNA polymerase activity?
What is a key reason negative-sense RNA viruses carry an RNA-dependent RNA polymerase in the virion?

options.A: Host ribosomes cannot translate genomic RNA directly
options.B: They require reverse transcriptase for integration
options.C: They package DNA-dependent DNA polymerase instead

items.stem: Which statement best describes the HCV life cycle entry step?

options.A: Attachment and receptor-mediated endocytosis followed by fusion
options.C: Budding from the nuclear membrane only

items.stem: How do many RNA viruses avoid detection by innate immunity early in infection?

options.A: Shielding or modifying viral RNA so RIG-I-like sensors are not triggered
options.D: Stopping host transcription globally within seconds

items.stem: Which genome structure is characteristic of influenza A virus?
What distinguishes retroviruses from most other RNA viruses?

options.A: Reverse transcription of RNA into DNA before integration
options.B: Direct translation of negative-sense RNA without polymerase
options.D: Replication solely in the nucleus without capsids

items.stem: Which host factor is commonly associated with lipid droplets during HCV assembly?

options.A: Core protein interactions organizing particle assembly

items.stem: Why is error-prone replication an evolutionary advantage for many RNA viruses?

options.A: It increases quasispecies diversity under selection pressure
options.B: It guarantees faithful repair of all mutations
options.C: It eliminates the need for any polymerase

sections.heading: Study and Revision Guidance
sections.content:
- Re-read the knowledge summary before attempting the self-check.
- Note one misconception you corrected while studying.
- Use the item rationales only after you have answered each question.
